threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newbies: they’re the ones trying to get on the bus at the wrong stop, oblivious to the four-minute walk to the train.
Caught the 68 last night; a guy tried to serenade the whole bus with an acoustic guitar. Only ended when someone threw a half-eaten burger at him.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I saw a spontaneous street party outside my window, with kids and old folks dancing together. That’s when it clicked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a doner kebab at 4 AM after a night out, but only in Kreuzberg will they serve it with a side of techno beats from the club next door.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know the taco truck schedules by heart but still hesitate at the BART closure drama.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo trying to woo a lady on the 31. She looked unimpressed, but he kept talking about how he knows 'the best view' in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars serve paella. In Gràcia, it’s all about tapas and vermut. Good luck finding a decent paella here!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones marveling at the bus driver’s route, still clutching that ancient metro map like a lifeline. You can see the confusion on their faces.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus like it's actually coming on time, instead of just accepting you'll be late no matter what.
Last night on the 59, a group of lads argued over who sang the best '90s anthem, nearly missed their stop at the Ritzy. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a half-finished döner in the park, wrapped in a real Berliner Zeitung, I thought, 'Yep, this is home.'

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a skate park inside an old brewery, where skaters and brewers mingle over craft beers and concrete ramps.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals have a sixth sense for dodging the never-ending pile of dog crap on Valencia Street. Tourists just step right in it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day, saw a saxophonist playing on the 49 bus—his solo drowned out by a dog barking at the stop. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘Gràcia’ is just about the parks and squares. They miss the little corner bakeries where locals discuss everything from politics to football.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by how they still ask for 'una tapa' at local bars instead of just grabbing what’s on the counter.
